metadata.title: Build a Five-Element Specification Generator for Codex Prompts
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: NO RENDERABLE BEATS: no beat carries a shot.remotion.pattern or Manim annotation — the pipeline will render NOTHING and every slot becomes a YOU-slate. The sheet was authored without the scene contract.
B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Every element of the spec replaces a default that would otherwise be wrong.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. Every element of the spec replaces a default that would otherwise be wrong.

beats.narration_text: 'Add user authentication' is an invitation for Codex to make decisions you didn't know you were delegating — which authentication library, which session model, which files to touch. A five-element specification makes every decision explicit: specific task, invariants, context, output format, negative constraints.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: 'Add user authentication' is an invitation for Codex to make decisions you didn't know you were delegating — which authentication library, which session model,

beats.narration_text: claude "Rewrite this task description as a five-element Codex specification. Element 1: Specific task. Element 2: Invariants. Element 3: Context. Element 4: Output format. Element 5: Negative constraints." < task.txt

beats.narration_text: spec_generator.py — converts natural-language task to five-element spec, runs weak request and spec through Codex, shows output diff.

props.code:
# converts task to five-element spec and compares outputs
import spec_engine, codex_runner, diff_reporter

task = open('task.txt').read()
weak_output = codex_runner.run(task)
spec = spec_engine.generate(task)
spec_output = codex_runner.run(spec)
diff_reporter.show(weak_output, spec_output)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: spec_generator.py — converts natural-language task to five-element spec, runs weak request and spec through Codex, shows output diff.

beats.narration_text: Weak request — 'add user authentication': Codex touches 7 files, introduces flask-login, creates a global session object. Five-element spec: Codex touches 2 files, no new dependencies, session stored in the existing User model. The spec didn't make Codex smarter — it made Codex specific to this codebase instead of to the average Flask project.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Weak request — 'add user authentication': Codex touches 7 files, introduces flask-login, creates a global session object. Five-element spec: Codex touches 2 fil

beats.narration_text: Remove the Negative constraints element and rerun the spec.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Remove the Negative constraints element and rerun the spec.

beats.narration_text: Without Negative constraints, Codex introduces a global session variable — one of the explicitly prohibited patterns. The 'do not use global state' constraint was doing real work — its removal immediately produced the pattern it was designed to block. The negative constraints are the most load-bearing element for any task with dangerous defaults.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Without Negative constraints, Codex introduces a global session variable — one of the explicitly prohibited patterns. The 'do not use global state' constraint w

beats.narration_text: The five-element spec is a delegation contract. Every element replaces a default: without Specific task, Codex picks its own scope; without Invariants, it modifies whatever it wants; without Negative constraints, it uses its training defaults. The spec is what makes Codex specific to your situation.
